originalAlarm.description: Result of 'malloc' is converted to a pointer of type '__ethtable_t_1885', which is incompatible with sizeof operand type 'struct (anonymous struct at bc0ffc0e971.desugared.c:2715:1)'

sanitizedDescription: Result of 'malloc' is converted to a pointer of type 'ethtable_t', which is incompatible with sizeof operand type 'struct (anonymous struct at bc0ffc0e971.desugared.c:2715:1)'

reasoning: bc0ffc0e971.c L29: MallocSizeof alarm on `ch = malloc(sizeof(*ch))`. PC simplifies to !__STRICT_ANSI__ AND !_STDLIB_H AND !__malloc_and_calloc_defined (the __need___FILE branches cancel out). All conditions are stdlib.h internal library macros, no feature config flags. Same desugaring artifact as TRANSFORMATION_14 (struct renaming in desugared file causes sizeof type mismatch) but with !__STRICT_ANSI__ instead of __STRICT_ANSI__.
